title: Mission Report Ep. 04 | Antigravity Updates
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It integrates AI agents directly with code editors, terminals, and browsers to enable autonomous development task execution.
writeup: Episode 4 of Google's Antigravity mission report covers the full surface expansion launched around Google I/O: a standalone Antigravity 2.0 desktop app built for orchestrating multiple agents across projects, a new CLI for terminal-centric workflows, a Python SDK for custom agent builds, and continued IDE support. The through-line is a single unified agent harness co-optimized with Gemini models that spans every surface, targeting not just developers but researchers, marketers, and general knowledge workers.
key_points: Antigravity 2.0 introduces parallel subagents for focused subtasks, asynchronous background task management to keep the UI responsive, cron-style scheduled recurring tasks, and voice transcription, representing the team's first dedicated agent-optimized desktop surface.

The Antigravity CLI preserves all core features from Gemini CLI (agent skills, hooks, subagents, extensions now called plugins) and adds single-command import of conversations into Antigravity 2.0, targeting developers who prefer a fast terminal experience with the same underlying intelligence.

The Antigravity SDK lets Python developers invoke the full agent harness via a pip install, with automatic management of memory, tool execution, and safety guardrails, so custom agent development focuses entirely on behavior and skills rather than infrastructure.

A science skills bundle integrates with over 30 major scientific databases and specialized alpha models; Google DeepMind used it for protein analysis workflows, shrinking complex multi-step processes from hours to minutes.

A stress-test project pushed Antigravity paired with Gemini 3.5 Flash to autonomously build an operating system from scratch, recreate the AlphaZero paper, and produce MVPs for multiple complex applications, all successfully completed.

Antigravity 2.0 is expanding beyond traditional developers to general knowledge work: Google marketers are using it to research, synthesize information, and generate new ideas, and Google Cloud enterprise customers can connect directly under existing enterprise terms.
